situation: Worried about my daughter abd her academic struggles. She has a genetic condition and some learning differences. Struggling to cope to work and do day to day things and give her as much support as she needs.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Hello. How are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Hi, I'm struggling today.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I'm sorry to hear that. Anything in particular that is bothering you?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I just feel worn out with everything I have to do.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I don't know what else I can d to help my daughter.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I understand that you are feeling worn out and don't know what else you can do to help your child. Is there something specific going on or is it just life in general?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: My daughter struggles with school work and memory. She learns differently and needs lots of support. I have contacted the school a number of times over the years but never really had any support or acknowledgement from them. I guess it's just an accumulation of things.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You have tried hard to help her and that is admirable. Don't beat yourself up and be proud of the things you have done for her.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: But what do I now. She's 17 and it's so difficult to give her the time and then  encourage her to tell me when she needs help and for her to be willing to accept it.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: While I do not have kids, from what I have seen my friends and family do in this situation is try to find something that your daughter is interested in and attempt to build off of that. So instead of her learning to just learn, she is learning something that is relevant to her. Maybe that might spark something in her to come up with her way of learning since traditional methods aren't working.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That is great advice. She does climb and hasn't been able to climb or see her friends with everything closed. I think as she will be able to again soon may be that will help her to relax and get back into it. She does learn through repetition but has lacked enthusiasm especially in the last few months.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Having her go climbing more sounds like a good plan. Hopefully things are opening up around you now that the vaccine is becoming more common.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Repetition is tough. I know I used a lot of flash cards as a way of learning through high-school and college. It's an old method but it can work.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: She had heart  surgery as a baby so has actually had the vaccine herself as she's in the vulnerable category.  I think she ends up spending so long making aide memoires that she then leaves herself so little time to actually study.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I can imagine after having heart surgery as a baby she wants to live life to it's fullest extent and prioritizes making memories over school. Does she ever talk about what her plans are for after high-school? At some point she will have to connect the dots of having to get a job to be able to go out and have fun, and unfortunately doing OK in school comes with that. That or a vocational school to learn a trade (what I personally did).

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: What did you do? Her interests are music and photography. Recently she has started a gardening club with the horticulture department at school. She does enjoy her social life well when she has been able to have it. She has had social ups and downs but apart from the epidemic she has been happier than in her earlier years at school. You are right she is becoming an adult and will find her place. At 17 I guess she doesn't have to decide just yet.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: You have certainly made me feel a bit more positive.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Sorry for the delay. I am glad you are feeling more positive. I was into cars so I went to vocational school to become an auto mechanic. After a couple years of that I realized I wasn't cut out for physical labor and went to college. But the knowledge from working on cars helped me understand things better at college since I had real world applications I could use it on.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Gardening and Horticulture is a great hobby! That definitely could cross over into some of her science classes to make things more interesting and relevant to her.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thank you so much for your time and kindness today. You helped me refocus and some of the things you said were just what I needed to hear.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Glad I was able to help. Sometimes just having someone to bounce things off of makes a big difference. You have a good heart and good intentions. I wish you and your daughter the best.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thank you.  Take care :-)

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You're welcome. It was my pleasure.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Please hit the quit button and take the survey. I can not end the chat from my side. Thank you!

dialog.content: And, if you do have a talk with him and later it becomes clear the relationship still can't work, it won't hit him as such a surprise when you choose to end things.

situation: My friend's husband is spreading false rumors about me because my daughter moved up in soccer and his didn't. He is saying I'm having an affair with the coach, who is married!  I am NOT and my daughter happens to be a much better soccer player.  I do like his wife but this is unforgiveable.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: what should I do?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Can you tell me a little more about your situation so I can better understand?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: My friend's husband is spreading false rumors about me because my daughter moved up in soccer and theirs didn't.  He's telling people it's because I'm having an affair with the coach - who is married!  This is not true!  My daughter is a better soccer player.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: what do you think I should do?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content:
It sounds like your friend's husband might be jealous of your daughter, or embarrassed
 that his daughter did not get moved up also. Have you spoken to your friend about this?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I haven't yet.  I was deciding whether or not to talk to him directly or just her or both at the same time.  I am really upset and don't want to lose her.  I know people get all crazy about their kids and sports.  I agree that he's jealous but his daughter isn't really that good - didn't make the school team either.  Should I talk to my friend alone first?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: It depends on the depth of the relationship between the three of you. How long have you all been friends?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: About 6 years.  Our daughters are also friends, too.  I am single so I don't care about the husband but also don't want to come in between their marriage.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Yes, that is a difficult situation to know how to handle. It could be in your best interest to leave your friend out of it so she doesn't feel stuck in the middle or forced to take sides. However she could view the opposite as you going behind her back.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: yes that's true.  I don't want to go behind her back but also don't want to get her involved.  I could have the coach talk to the dad maybe?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Yes, coaches are a great resource for life's dilemmas. Perhaps another option would be to casually mention something to him in passing, like at one your daughters games as to avoid any big blowups or emotional exlosions.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Oh that's a good idea - in public and not making a big deal about it - and before she moves teams and rumors get worse.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That is exactly right. Each person handles and avoids conflict differently and you have the important roll as leader in all of this. There is a chance he is not aware of the strain this has put on you emotionally and he could respond well to not being called out by someone else. Does that make sense?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: it does - but doesn't seem that he cares about my strain on my life since he's the one spreading such lies about me.  I just hate him!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I can understand how you would feel like he doesn't care about the strain it's placed. Can I ask, are your daughters aware or involved in these rumor's? This could make a difference in how you could respond.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I did tell her because I was so mad when another mom told me.  She doesn't care.  She still likes the friend but not the dad.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Sounds like your daughter has a good head on her shoulders. Maybe there's something to be learned from her reaction to all of this. I understand how hard this has been for you so perhaps simply ignoring the rumors will allow you to heal quicker, by simply being the bigger person. Because rumors are just rumors, after all. You and your daughter know the truth and that's all that matters.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: yes you're right.  I will try to just ignore.  And figure out what to do with my anger over all this?  Maybe go for a run and also avoid him?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: If he knows you know and you rise above with a smile on your face, that could show him that you are more mature than his childish games, and that you mean business. By taking ownership of your own feelings and emotions, you might find that will squash your need to let out any anger towards him.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: oh I like that and I am willing to try.  I am tired of wasting my energy being so angry at this man who like you said is jealous which is HIS problem and shouldn't be might, right?!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content:
Exactly. Let his problems remain his. You worry about you and your daughter and he can watch from a distance confused at his failed attempt to reflect his own dissatisfaction with life onto you didn't work. I think you will feel freed
 of any stress this has caused.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I really like that and I hope I'm able to do that!! Thank you!

situation: My (ex)fiancé and I recently broke up, but we're still spending a great deal of time together, and we both say we're not sure if our relationship is 100% "done" and agreed to tell each other if we start dating other people. I wouldn't have come in had I known what I'd find inside.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content:

im yet?

situation: My manager has decided that everyone will continue to work from home nearly 100% of the time after the pandemic is under control. (Prior to the pandemic, most of us only had one work-from-home day a week.) I really love the work I do, but I've been experiencing depression this past year largely due to my lack of human contact throughout the work day.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Hi, how are you? What would you like to talk about today?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Hi - I'm sorry. I stepped away from the computer for a moment while waiting...

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That's quite all right. The wait times can be a bit long. How are you?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Well, my manager has decided that everyone in the office will continue to work from after the pandemic is under control. But I've found myself depressed from working from home all the time.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Working from home is not what people expected. Many have found themselves isolated. They miss the interaction of the office. How long have you bee working from home?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: For a full year--since March 2020, when the pandemic first started. I was working from home just 1 day per week prior to that, which I liked. But I've found that 5 days per week is much too much. I miss people. :(

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Have you and your co-workers talked to your boss about a hybrid schedule, maybe a couple days a week in the office? That might work.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That's where it gets awkward. It seems like everyone besides me are super happy that we'll continue working from home. I hesitate to share my feelings because maybe my manager will change her mind, which would be a big bummer for everyone else on the team (seemingly).

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I see. That's a concern. So you feel that if one person goes in to the office, then everyone else will have to? There won't be any flexibility?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Well, it's possible that my manager will say we can be flexible. But if so, I think I'd end up being the only one in my office working... which would be just as lonely as working from home while throwing away the few perks of working from home, like saving on gas. Plus, I think my company will probably want to repurpose our office space eventually.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: lol, sorry, that was an ugly run-on sentence. :\

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Hm. A bit of a dilemma. Can this situation go on forever though? Maybe the boss will extend the stay-at-home for a while, but when other companies go back to the office, the expectation will be there for all companies.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Other employees might change their minds bit by bit as well.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That is indeed a possibility. But my office is in a city that has a whole lot of high-tech companies, which were moving towards doing more work from home even BEFORE the pandemic. So it kind of feels like this might be a "cultural shift" in my area in general. Although there has GOT to be a whole lot of other people who feel like I do. Maybe we should start our own company, lol.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: True. I don't think you are alone. Is there something that you can do to make working at home better for you? More frequent breaks? Better work space?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That's a good question. My state is still one of the ones that's mostly shut-down. Part of my depression surely stems from the fact that all evening/weekend activities ended a year ago as well.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: True. It might be easier to work at home when you have a chance to get out of the house during your free time.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That's true. And your suggestion of improving my work space is a good one. I've been working in a modified walk-in closet. I've tried to make it inviting, but there are no windows/etc.... it's kind of a dark cave.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Most people didn't plan for the long term because nobody thought that this was a sustainable way to work. By the way, if your company wants you to stay home, then it might help buy the equipment you need to be productive.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That's a good thought. My computer is set-up on a folding card table. (I tried finding a desk a while back, but there was a huge shortage since everyone suddenly had to buy them for themselves, kids, etc.). I hadn't thought of asking my company to buy me one. Plus a more comfy chair could improve my attitude.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I think if you talk to your co-workers, they'll have ideas for upgrading your workspace.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That's a great idea. Maybe I should set-up a "virtual happy hour" where we can exchanged the tips/tricks we've learned to make working from home more enjoyable this past year.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I think that's a great idea! People probably want to connect without having to talk about work. Later on, they might want to do the same thing but face-to-face.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: You're right! Well, thank you so much for your help.... and you TRULY did help me put this all into perspective. Sincerely, thanks! :)

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Sure! My pleasure. Best of luck with all that! Have a great weekend!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thanks! You too! Bye

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Is there a quit/finish button on your end?

yes it does get alot more complicated exp

situation: I wish I had a girlfriend. I haven't seen any friends outside of my household since last October!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Hello. How are you? What would you like to talk about?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Got any advice for a lonely single fellow in his mid thirties? I miss my friends, and virtual meetings on Zoom don't feel the same as meeting in person!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: The pandemic has been hard on many people, those in relationships and those looking for new ones! Have you tried to develop a new hobby or interest that you can do with others once the pandemic is over?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Well, I've been writing enough poetry to publish a book, so I'll certainly do that. I know the pandemic has been rough for everyone. Hopefully my doctor will say I qualify for the vaccine soon.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Getting my stimulus check in the next week or so would certainly make me a bit happier, too.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: That's great! Poetry is something you can share in person or over video link. What about physical activities? I think that yoga might be something that you can do by yourself and with others. There are a bunch of videos on sites like YouTube.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: A stimulus check! Yes, we can all use money during these trying times.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Oh, I took advantage of virtual yoga and zumba early on during the pandemic. Friends had virtual classes. Eventually they were able to go back to work. Being disabled is a poverty trap for a lot of people, not just me. I really want to find a girlfriend once I get vaccinated.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I have tried yoga myself, but it wasn't really my thing. I prefer walking and riding a bike.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Do you have to wait until you get vaccinated to start working on getting a girlfriend?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Well, certain things don't work for every person.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: True. I like trying new things, but I am happy to give them up if they just don't fit my style.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Is there a way you could start looking for a girlfriend online now, before the pandemic is over?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Maybe there is a way to make a connection using your poetry.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Technically, no, I don't HAVE to wait to get vaccinated, but then again, maybe I don't want a girlfriend. The only one I had ten years ago in college wanted to marry me and have a child and I wasn't ready for commitment. I fear marriage, actually.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Marriage is a big step, and any prudent person would think carefully before making the commitment.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That's a good point, I'm sure if my poetry is entertaining or full of truth about my experiences in a wheelchair, perhaps a woman will find that attractive

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Are you concerned that things will move too quickly to that level of commitment (marriage)? You could make it clear from the outset that you want to take it slow.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Or I could just get an adult entertainer to be my companion for a day. I've done that before with women in that industry. It costs a bit, but it's worth it for companionship

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: No, I just hate the idea of waking up to one woman for 20 or 30 years

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: But it's better than being alone

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Robin Williams talked about that situation.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: He said that he didn't fear being alone as much as being with people that he didn't like.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Did he really? Well, it's sad how he ended up, I'm too scared to commit suicide like he did! I enjoy living life, even when I have months at a time without human connection

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Adult entertainers provide one kind of companionship, but it is not complete. It feels like a stopgap to me. Is it fulfilling enough for you?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: You're right, I just lack self confidence in my poetry....hopefully I'll figure out a way to spit it out on a blog or something

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You could trade poems with other budding poets. "I'll show you mine if you show me yours."

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Do you find yourself to be more an introvert or an extrovert?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Oh it's been very fulfilling for me when I've had the opportunity to use it. Most of them are genuine women. Only a handful have tried to take advantage of me.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I'm definitely an extrovert

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That was a clever pun by the way

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: It's ironic and strange how many people struggle with that stupid voice in their head that tells them they're not good enough to accomplish anything.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Thanks. Ok. The pandemic is particularly hard on extroverts. People look down on sex workers, but I think that the world is a big complex place and people are trying to make their way in it. If everyone is consenting, I see no problem.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Have you tried psilocybin? Some people swear that it changes the inner monologue for the better. I am not a physician, but for long-term adjustment, it might be beneficial.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Well of course it's consentual. I would never NOT ask for permission to do things with a sex worker, or any person.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I'll have to Google that, I've never heard of that, but thanks for the advice!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Sure! I'm glad we had a chance to chat. Best of luck with making friends/girlfriends after the pandemic!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: BTW, is there a quit button on your end?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yes there is, and I am pressing it right now. Thanks so much, I'll be fine. Have a good night!

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Good night!

situation: I have to give up my parrot because I am now blind and have to move.  I cant take teh parrot with me.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Hi, can I help you today?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I have had my babu for 15 years and have to give him up.  He is a parrot and so used to me.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I am so sorry to hear that. You have probably become very attached after that long, I can imagine.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yes, I am worried that he wont be taken care of.  I am worried that he wont be happy.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I cook for him and take him outside.  I grow fruit so he can eat it.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Can I ask why you have to give him up? Do you have any other pets?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: NO other pets.  I have gone leaglly blind, had to give up driving.  I have to move to a condo from a single famuly home.  He is too loud to live in a condo.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You are a very good caretaker for your parrot. You have given him a lot of specialized care.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Going blind is bad enough, giving up driving was teh worst.  But giving up my parrot is really hard.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I did baby him, he can go outside through the dggie door.  He gets fresh fruit, spaghettie,  He has his own Alexa.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: You have been the best you could be for him and it has got to feel really sad to let him go. It can feel like a death sometimes when you lose someone or a pet.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: If he died it would be OK, I would mourn him and move on.  But I have never had to give up a pet before.  I just think most people. wont take care of him like I do. Because he is still alive, I will worry about him

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I understand a little bit of how you feel. My dog died recently and it was really hard when the vet had to put him down. I was really sad and would cry when I thought of him ort the things we used to do together.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yes, I was depressed for 6 months after my parrot died, but eventually we move on.  I dont know that I can move on after giving him up

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Would you ever consider getting a pet that is not as loud? I am not suggesting that you replace him.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: No, except a seeing dog

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I am concerned that if you don't have a pet to care for you may have a more difficult time to recover and thrive. Mourning is a process and it might be easier if you can hare your ;love with another pet.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: No other pets.  I need to move and cannot take him with me.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: A seeing eye dog is a great idea. You are trying to think of ways to help yourself and that is really important. You are also reaching out and sharing your feelings. Do you have any friends with pets that you can visit with from time to time?

dialog.content: It isn't about a new pet it is about worrying that he will not be taken care of.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I contacted a rescue place to get him a new home.

dialog.content: I am sorry that makes a lot of sense. I am really thankful that you are sharing your feelings and expressing them.

dialog.content: Contacting a rescue place has helped me.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Rescue centers usually make people apply to adopt to insure a good home for them. You have made him into a wonderful pet.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I think that will be best for him

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thanks for listening

dialog.content: You are welcome. You are doing what is right and i am proud of you for listening to your heart and providing the best all these years.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Thank you for your time today and please come back whenever you need to talk. I am happy to listen and do my best.

dialog.content: I see many friends getting relying on the medication even after they feel OK.

dialog.content: Sure. Everyone pays a small percentage of their income - depending on how much they earn - and it goes to fund the NHS. Everyone is therefore eligible for free hospital treatment etc and we pay a standard amount (about ten pounds ie fourteen dollars) for a prescription, no matter which drugs we are gettign

dialog.content: If I needed a heart transplant tomorrow it would be free :D

dialog.content: Most of Europe do it that way. It works well :)

dialog.content: However, the US healthcare system is totally different, so commercialized.

dialog.content: that's why I look for supporters today.

dialog.content: sure thing!

situation: I'm a therapist and I'm getting burned out on the job.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Hi, how are you?

dialog.content: Hello there, how are you doing today?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content:
I'm ok. To be honest, 
I'm a therapist and I'm getting burned out on the job.

dialog.speaker: assistant

dialog.speaker: user

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Are you in a financial position where you could take some time off, go travelling perhaps, and renew yourself?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I've thought about that. I'd love to travel. I feel an obligation to my patients. I don't want to abandon them. I could pass them to other therapists, maybe temporarily. I don't know how they would feel about that.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Well, I would hope that they would understand that even therapists have to have a private life. And I am sure they'd prefer you at the peak of your game and not burned out.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: True. They might wonder how committed I am to them and to the profession if I go away for a while. I've thought about lightening my work load too.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Yes perhaps you could switch to part time? If you could afford it?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Money not really the issue. I just worry that I'll keep burning out, just more slowly. Other therapists go through this as well.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: Somewhere at the top of the chain there must a very weary therapist with no-one to talk to. Though I suppose that is where these bots come in :D

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Yeah. One day I'll be replaced by a computer. Then the computers will provide therapy to other computers, who'll need a vacation when they burn out.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: seriously though, I guess I could try part-time for a while and see how it goes.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: lol. It's an interesting mental picture. I am glad you still have a sense of humour though

dialog.content: Yeah - try  and take up a new hobby with the extra time, maybe?

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I am sorry to hear that. On another note, have you considered speakign to your GP? Perhaps medication might help.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: My therapist recommended medication too, but I worry that it might impair my ability to do my job.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I have tried medication and I found it surprisingly helpful but everyone is different of course. There was no impairment though.

dialog.speaker: assistant

situation: My best friend's son died in a car accident.

dialog.content: I'm really sorry to hear that. When did this happen?

dialog.content: The accident was Thursday February 25th but he held on until Sunday the 28th

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: He was only 26 years old

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: There's nothing that can prepare you for unexpected tragedies like this. We just have to deal with them as they come.

dialog.content: I feel helpless because there is nothing I can do to make her feel better.

dialog.content: It sounds like you're sticking close to your friend, although it doesn't feel like its helping.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: It's my experience that when someone has experienced a traumatic loss like that, making someone feel better, at least right away, isn't always an option.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: The most important thing you can do for her is just to be there for her and allow her to feel however she needs to feel right now.

dialog.speaker: assistant

dialog.speaker: assistant

situation: School's going badly. I really wish I had chosen a different major.

dialog.speaker: user

dialog.content: I've thought about take a leave of absence, switching majors, going to trade school, traveling abroad...

dialog.content: What about meeting with the counselor with your parents, since it seems they would need to support you financially over any extra time it might take?

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: That's a good point. Maybe we could have a Zoom meeting, even for just 15 minutes. I need to get my parents to understand my feelings.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: The counselor might help me with that.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: It's been shown that healthy communication can help people listen and respond more considerately when things seem difficult at first.

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: I hadn't thought of it that way. I'm so worried about schoolwork that I hadn't stepped back from the whole situation of school itself.

dialog.speaker: assistant
dialog.content: I definitely can understand that, but it is important to think about your future happiness and security too!

dialog.speaker: user
dialog.content: Thanks again for all your help! Your words have been really insightful!
